# Screener Weekly — 2026-06-27

*Generated by the Saturday-morning routine. Source data: live `/api/screener/morning/run` against the production Railway deployment.*

## TL;DR

**API failure — no trade data available this week (ninth consecutive week).** The proxy in the claude.ai/code routines execution environment rejects outbound CONNECT tunnels to `trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app` with HTTP 403 (`gateway answered 403 to CONNECT — policy denial`). No ticker data was generated; nothing in this report should be used for Monday trade prep until the egress issue is resolved.

## API Failure Details

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Endpoint | `POST /api/screener/morning/run` |
| Proxy error | `gateway answered 403 to CONNECT (policy denial or upstream failure)` |
| Universe Endpoint | `GET /api/screener/universe?mode=swing` |
| Failure type | Outbound egress blocked by `https_proxy` at `127.0.0.1:42641` |
| Note | Ninth consecutive Saturday blocked — 2026-04-26, 2026-05-02, 2026-05-16, 2026-05-23, 2026-05-30, 2026-06-06, 2026-06-13, 2026-06-20, **2026-06-27** |

## Caveats / known gaps

- **API blocked (proxy 403) — ninth consecutive week:** The production Railway deployment at `https://trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app` is unreachable because the `https_proxy` at `127.0.0.1:42641` returns `HTTP 403 Forbidden` to the CONNECT tunnel request — a policy denial at the network layer before the request reaches Railway. The proxy's `recentRelayFailures` log confirms `kind: "connect_rejected"` and `detail: "gateway answered 403 to CONNECT (policy denial or upstream failure)"`.
- Plan 1 only ships Coiled Spring — other scans (Pradeep 4%, Qullamaggie EP, Saty Triggers, Vomy, etc.) are deferred to Plan 2.
- `backfill_days_in_compression` is not yet wired into the runner — newly-detected coils start at day 1 even if they've been coiled for weeks. Plan 2 task.
- The compression proxy (rolling stddev/SMA20) is a stand-in for the real Phase Oscillator. Plan 2 will replace it.

## Root cause (unchanged from prior weeks)

The routines execution environment runs all outbound HTTPS through a local proxy (`HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:42641`). That proxy has an egress allowlist; `*.up.railway.app` is not on it. Every CONNECT tunnel attempt to the Railway host is rejected with 403 before any data flows.

This is confirmed by the proxy status endpoint:

```json
{
  "kind": "connect_rejected",
  "detail": "gateway answered 403 to CONNECT (policy denial or upstream failure)",
  "host": "trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app:443"
}
```

**The restriction is on the claude.ai/code routines runner side, not on Railway.** The fix must be applied in the Claude Code web environment — `*.up.railway.app` must be added to the environment's outbound network allowlist.

## Action required (escalated — nine consecutive failures)

To unblock next Saturday's run, **one of**:

1. **Add `*.up.railway.app` to the routines environment's egress allowlist** in the claude.ai/code environment network settings. This is the correct fix. See: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web.
2. **Proxy the Railway API behind a domain already on the allowlist** (e.g., a custom domain on Vercel/Cloudflare pointing to the Railway service) — adds complexity but unblocks without needing environment config changes.
